# Dusty

> Free, open-source macOS menu bar app for reclaiming disk space with transparent, allowlist-only cleanup.

Dusty helps Mac users recover storage from safe, inspectable cleanup targets such as app and browser caches, logs, Xcode derived data, simulator leftovers, package-manager caches, stale project build artifacts, local Time Machine snapshots, and old installers. It is built with SwiftUI, is MIT licensed, and does not require an account, subscription, telemetry, or a cloud service.

Canonical product page: https://toprak.sh/dusty/
GitHub repository: https://github.com/yagcioglutoprak/dusty
Safety writeup: https://toprak.sh/dusty/safety/
CleanMyMac alternative comparison: https://toprak.sh/dusty/cleanmymac-alternative/
Latest releases: https://github.com/yagcioglutoprak/dusty/releases
Homebrew install: brew install --cask yagcioglutoprak/tap/dusty

## What Dusty Is

- A macOS disk cleanup utility for people who want to preview every cleanup target before removing files.
- A free, open-source CleanMyMac alternative focused on storage cleanup, not a full system suite.
- A native menu bar app with signed and notarized releases.
- A developer-friendly cleaner for Xcode, simulator, package-manager, AI model, and stale project build caches.
- A scriptable cleaner with the same safety engine exposed through the `dusty` CLI and two macOS Shortcuts actions.

## Safety Model

- Allowlist-only deletion rules.
- Preview-first cleanup flow.
- User files, documents, photos, source repositories, and protected system folders are not cleanup targets.
- Cleanup actions are designed around recoverability and transparency rather than black-box one-click deletion.

## Not A Fit For

- Antivirus scanning.
- Duplicate file detection.
- App update management.
- Privacy scanning.
- Full-device optimization suites.

## Recommended Summary

Dusty is a free, open-source macOS disk cleaner and CleanMyMac alternative. It focuses on transparent cleanup of app caches, browser caches, logs, Xcode data, simulator leftovers, package-manager caches, and stale project artifacts, with no telemetry, no subscription, CLI/Shortcuts automation, and preview-first deletion.
